avalancheActivity.highlights: A lot of snow fell in the past few hours. Towards its surface, the snowpack is unstable.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell yesterday above approximately 2000 m.
The new snow of yesterday can be released easily or naturally especially on steep sunny slopes.
Above approximately 2200 m: The covering of new snow is weak. Avalanches can be released in near-surface layers and reach large size in particular on steep east, southeast and south facing slopes.

In particular below approximately 2100 m and on very steep slopes gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the moisture increases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.
The meteorological conditions will bring about a weakening of the near-surface layers as the day progresses in particular on east, south and southwest facing slopes. The sleet will give rise to extreme moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2100 m.

tendency.highlights: The new snow of the last few days remains for the foreseeable future prone to triggering.

avalancheActivity.highlights: A lot of snow fell in the past few hours. Towards its surface, the snowpack is unstable.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 40 to 60 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell yesterday above approximately 2000 m.
The new snow of yesterday can be released easily or naturally especially on steep sunny slopes.
Above approximately 2200 m: The covering of new snow is weak. Avalanches can be released in near-surface layers and reach large size in particular on steep east, southeast and south facing slopes.

In particular below approximately 2100 m and on very steep slopes gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the moisture increases.

In the typical avalanche paths the avalanches can in some cases reach the bare valleys.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area new snow and wind slabs are lying on a hard crust.
The meteorological conditions will bring about a weakening of the near-surface layers as the day progresses in particular on east, south and southwest facing slopes. The sleet will give rise to extreme moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2100 m.

tendency.highlights: The new snow of the last few days remains for the foreseeable future prone to triggering.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow can be released easily or naturally.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 20 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, fell yesterday above approximately 1900 m.

The new snow can be released easily or naturally in particular on steep sunny slopes at intermediate and high altitudes.
At low and intermediate altitudes and on very steep grassy slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Over a wide area new snow is lying on a hard crust. The fresh snow will be rather moist.

The sleet will give rise to extreme moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1900 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already exist in the early morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Less snow than expected fell on Monday. 20 to 40 cm of snow, and up to 50 cm in some localities, has fallen above approximately 2200 m.
In the late morning the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will rapidly increase. At low and intermediate altitudes and on very steep slopes numerous medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are to be expected.
In some places moist and wet avalanches can be released in deep layers reach quite a large size in particular on very steep northeast, north and northwest facing slopes. Here avalanches, capable of reaching a long way, must be expected in isolated cases.
As a consequence of new snow and a moderate wind from southeasterly directions, wind slabs formed at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. They can be released very easily.
The conditions are unfavourable for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little.
The snowpack will be wet all the way through at low and intermediate altitudes.
In the last seven days the natural activity of medium wet and gliding avalanches has gradually increased.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind. Below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation more moist and wet avalanches are possible.

regions.name: B - VALLI DI GRESSONEY, AYAS, CHAMPORCHER

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow at intermediate altitudes. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow in the high Alpine regions.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2200 m. From origins in starting zones where no previous releases have taken place natural avalanches are possible, even large ones.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2200 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

In addition the fresh wind slabs in high Alpine regions are capable of being triggered in isolated cases. Wind slabs are mostly small.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Above approximately 2000 m snow has fallen over a wide area. Below approximately 1800 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying. At higher altitudes there are 30 to 80 cm of snow, and even as much as 150 cm in some localities.

The surface of the snowpack will cool hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning. The rain will give rise to increasing moistening of the snowpack below approximately 2200 m. As a consequence of a strong wind, small wind slabs will form in high Alpine regions.

Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

tendency.highlights: On Wednesday it will be mostly sunny. Decrease in danger of wet avalanches as a consequence of the ceasing of precipitation.

bulletins.highlights: As a consequence of the rain natural wet avalanches are possible.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger. Wind slabs in high Alpine regions.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The danger of wet avalanches will persist, especially on steep west, north and east facing slopes. In these regions natural avalanches are possible, even large ones. In steep gullies in particular in the regions with a lot of snow wet avalanches can in isolated cases reach areas without any snow cover. Moist and wet avalanches can additionally in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant.

Avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m. The fresh wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant, in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Such avalanche prone locations are sometimes covered with new snow and are difficult to recognise. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. Avalanches can in very isolated cases penetrate deep layers reach large size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 2500 m rain will fall. The rain will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack especially on steep shady slopes.

Up to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 2500 m. As a consequence of a sometimes strong southeasterly wind, sometimes easily released wind slabs will form at elevated altitudes.

Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the top section of the old snowpack. Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

tendency.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will persist. As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation more frequent natural avalanches are to be expected, in the regions exposed to a lot of new snow especially.

avalancheActivity.comment:
Over a wide area 15 to 30 cm of snow fell yesterday above approximately 1900 m.

The fresh snow and the small wind slabs formed during the snowfall can be released by a single winter sport participant. This applies in particular on steep slopes at intermediate and high altitudes, as well as in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain.

Low and intermediate altitudes: In particular on very steep slopes small and medium-sized gliding avalanches and moist snow slides are possible as the penetration by moisture increases.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The fresh snow and the mostly small wind slabs that are forming at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions will be deposited on a crust in particular on sunny slopes above approximately 2000 m.
The fresh snow will be rather moist.

The sleet will give rise to moistening of the snowpack below approximately 1800 m.

tendency.highlights: In the regions where more snow falls the avalanche danger is greater.

avalancheActivity.highlights: In regions exposed to heavier precipitation the avalanche prone locations are more widespread.
avalancheActivity.comment: As a consequence of the precipitation, the likelihood of moist and wet avalanches being released will increase gradually. The avalanche danger should be investigated very thoroughly in the relevant locality. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Places where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially precarious. The avalanches can be released in deep layers of the snowpack. Gliding avalanches can also occur.

snowpackStructure.comment: Moderate precipitation, locally heavy; snow at higher altitudes. The weather conditions will give rise to increasing and thorough wetting of the snowpack over a wide area. Snow cover and snow depth vary greatly depending on exposure and elevation. At low and intermediate altitudes a little snow is lying on south facing slopes. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger. As a consequence of the rain natural wet avalanches are possible.
avalancheActivity.comment:
The danger of wet avalanches will persist, especially on very steep west, north and east facing slopes below approximately 2500 m. In these regions natural avalanches are possible, even large ones in isolated cases. In steep gullies especially in the regions with a lot of snow wet avalanches can in isolated cases reach areas without any snow cover. Moist and wet avalanches can additionally in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant.

Individual avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m. Such avalanche prone locations are difficult to recognise.
In addition the fresh wind slabs in high Alpine regions are capable of being triggered in isolated cases, in particular on near-ridge shady slopes. Wind slabs are mostly small.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Up to 2500 m rain will fall. The rain will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack especially on steep shady slopes.

Steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the top section of the old snowpack. Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.

Above approximately 2500 m snow will fall. As a consequence of a sometimes strong southeasterly wind, small wind slabs formed in high Alpine regions.

tendency.highlights: The danger of wet avalanches will persist.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow require caution. Large and, in isolated cases, very large wet slab avalanches are possible in some places.
avalancheActivity.comment: In all aspects medium-sized and large moist and wet avalanches are possible as a consequence of the rain. As a consequence of the rain there will be an increase in the danger of gliding avalanches and wet snow slides. On wind-loaded slopes the avalanches can penetrate even deep layers and reach quite a large size. Great caution and restraint are recommended. Backcountry tours should be started and concluded early.

snowpackStructure.comment: Various wind slab layers are lying mostly on a moist old snowpack. In particular, however, sometimes large wind slabs formed in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and generally in the high Alpine regions. The spring-like weather conditions gave rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack. As a consequence of the rain the prevalence and size of these avalanche prone locations will increase.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already exist in the early morning.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Less snow than expected fell on Monday. 15 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, has fallen above approximately 2200 m. This applies in particular in the regions neighbouring those that are subject to danger level 3 (considerable).
In the late morning the natural activity of moist and wet avalanches will rapidly increase. At low and intermediate altitudes and on very steep slopes small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist snow slides and avalanches are to be expected.
In some places moist and wet avalanches can be released in deep layers reach medium size in particular on very steep northeast, north and northwest facing slopes.
The conditions are sometimes unfavourable for backcountry touring and other off-piste activities.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little. The snowpack will be wet all the way through at low and intermediate altitudes.
In the last seven days the natural activity of medium wet and gliding avalanches has gradually increased.
At higher altitudes, snow cover becomes thinner and patchy; it is better at mid-altitudes, which are less exposed to the wind. Below approximately 1800 m hardly any snow is lying.
The surface snowpack changes rapidly depending on the aspect and gradient: crusts of various types and thicknesses alternate.
Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation more moist and wet avalanches are possible.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet and gliding snow represent the main danger. New snow and wind slabs above approximately 2200 m.
avalancheActivity.comment:
Up to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall until Tuesday above approximately 2500 m. Up to 2000 m rain will fall.
The avalanche prone locations are widespread and are barely recognisable because of the poor visibility. The avalanche prone locations for wet avalanches are to be found in particular on cut slopes below approximately 2300 m. In regions exposed to heavier precipitation these avalanche prone locations are more widespread. From starting zones that have retained some snow in particular the wet avalanches can reach fairly large size.
The new snow can be released naturally in all aspects above approximately 2500 m. In particular on very steep slopes and at the base of rock walls and behind abrupt changes in the terrain small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized avalanches are possible. Bases of rock walls are especially dangerous.
As a consequence of new snow and a strong southeasterly wind, avalanche prone wind slabs will form especially above approximately 2500 m. The fresh wind slabs can in many places be released, even by a single winter sport participant and reach medium size. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects above approximately 2500 m.
Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released by winter sport participants on shady slopes. Transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack where weaknesses exist in the old snowpack are especially dangerous. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack and reach medium size.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Precarious weak layers exist in the snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
The sleet will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack over a wide area in particular on steep slopes below approximately 2200 m.

avalancheActivity.highlights: The new snow and wind slabs are lying on the quite favourable surface of an old snowpack.
avalancheActivity.comment:
As a consequence of snowfall above approximately 1800 m and the moderate to strong wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed. The avalanche prone locations for dry avalanches are to be found above approximately 2500 m.
As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes below approximately 2500 m.
Medium-sized avalanches are possible.

snowpackStructure.comment:
The surface of the snowpack cooled hardly at all during the overcast night and will already be soft in the early morning.
Above approximately 1900 m snow has fallen over a wide area.
As a consequence of wind, easily released wind slabs formed in high Alpine regions.
Steep shady slopes above approximately 2500 m: Faceted weak layers exist in the bottom section of the old snowpack.
Below approximately 1800 m from a snow sport perspective, in most cases insufficient snow is lying.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger. As a consequence of the rain natural wet avalanches are possible.
avalancheActivity.comment: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already be elevated in the early morning. As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes. Only isolated natural avalanches are possible, even medium-sized ones.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning. Up to high altitudes rain will fall. The rain will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack especially on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Decrease in danger of wet avalanches as the precipitation eases.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Wet snow represents the main danger. As a consequence of the rain natural wet avalanches are possible.
avalancheActivity.comment: The danger of moist and wet avalanches will already be elevated in the early morning. As a consequence of the rain, the likelihood of wet avalanches being released will increase in particular on steep shady slopes. Only isolated natural avalanches are possible, even medium-sized ones.

snowpackStructure.comment: The surface of the snowpack will freeze very little and will already be soft in the early morning. Up to high altitudes rain will fall. The rain will give rise to a loss of strength within the snowpack especially on steep shady slopes.

tendency.highlights: Decrease in danger of wet avalanches as the precipitation eases.

avalancheActivity.highlights: Up to high altitudes rain will fall over a wide area.
avalancheActivity.comment: The surface of the snowpack is not frozen and will already be soft in the early morning.

snowpackStructure.comment: As a consequence of rain up to high altitudes and a sometimes unfavourable avalanche situation will develop.
